meta.setting: raised ore trestle and chute deck over a rocky creek cut
meta.rdr_clone_target: PS3-era RDR-clone western art, grounded in mining logistics and height danger

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining and transport pressure in rugged terrain.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Mining-era industrial material and settlement context.

image_prompt.subject: a raised timber ore trestle with an ore car, chute, loose brake rope, workers on the deck, and a rider watching from below
image_prompt.scene: dusty afternoon; spilled ore rocks bounce down a chute, one worker points at a cracked brace, creek stones and scrub sit far beneath, no readable signs
image_prompt.composition: square height-danger scene with trestle diagonally crossing the frame, deck above, shadowed ground below, and figures at both levels
image_prompt.camera: low creek-bed angle looking up through trestle timbers, like a player hiding below a work site
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Make the structure look useful enough to trust and old enough to doubt.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD in-engine mining structure scene
render_traits: slightly simplified workers, rider, ore car, chute, and trestle geometry
modest texture resolution on wood grain, ore dust, iron, rope, cloth, and stone
hard ambient occlusion under braces, ore car, deck planks, chute, and figures
hot dusty light with dark under-structure shadows
believable 1899 mining infrastructure and work clothes
vertical playable composition with possible climb, hide, sabotage, and fall routes
style_modifiers.palette: ore gray, timber brown, dust ochre, iron black, rope tan, creek-stone blue, scrub green, and sun-bleached sky.

text_rendering.instructions: No readable warning boards, crate marks, painted names, or text.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos
scenic overlook with no work function

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The trestle, ore car, chute, rope, workers, rider below, and height are readable.
Every object belongs plausibly to 1899 mining logistics.
The image supports worksite, sabotage, chase, fall, and hidden-witness scenes.
No copied game characters, readable modern text, or inherited non-western symbols.
